title: How to Get a Police Certificate – Djibouti - Canada.ca
content:
## How to Get a Police Certificate – Djibouti
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.
Do I need to provide a Police Certificate with my Application for Permanent Residence? Yes
Name of document(s) to get: Bulletin N° 3 Extrait du casier judiciaire or Certificat de non condamnation (issued to foreigners/non-residents)

## How to get a Police Certificate:

### If you are a citizen of Djibouti:

#### If you live in Djibouti:
You should apply in person to the Greffier en Chef at the Ministère de la Justice (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring:
- Your national identity card

#### If you live outside of Djibouti:
You should apply to the Greffier en Chef at the Ministère de la Justice (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring:
- Copy of your national identity card
- Copy of your passport
- Names of both parents, date of birth and place of residence while in Djibouti

### If you are not a citizen of Djibouti:

#### If you live in Djibouti:
You should apply in person to the Greffier en Chef at the Ministère de la Justice (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring:
- Copy of your passport
- Identity card for foreign nationals

#### If you live outside of Djibouti:
You should apply to the Greffier en Chef at the Ministère de la Justice (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring:
- Copy of your passport  and copies of visa/permits showing the period of legal residence in Djibouti
- Copy of identity card for foreign nationals
- Names of both parents, date of birth and place of residence while in Djibouti

### Information and documents you need:
- All information and documents needed are explained above

### Special notes and considerations
Greffier en Chef Tribunal de Djibouti: Ministère de la Justice, des Affaires pénitentiaires et musulmanes Chargé des droits de l’homme Boulevard de la République B.P. 12 Djibouti, Republic of Djibouti
- Applying and collecting the certificate is done by the concerned applicant
- Fingerprints are not taken at the time of police certificate issuance for both Djibouti citizens and foreign nationals
- For Djibouti citizens fingerprints are taken when their national identity cards are issued
- For foreign nationals, fingerprints that were taken during the preparation of their resident permits are in their file
- Certificat de non condamnation is issued to foreigners/non-residents
